title: Privacy-first constituent case model + retention windows/deletion SLA + records-law-hold exemption
context: Constituent PII must be minimized by design. The casework model carries only service fields (issue, status, next step, outcome, minimal contact) -- no political/affiliation/profiling fields exist. PII is encrypted at rest. A default 24-month retention window auto-minimizes non-records data after case closure; constituent/official deletion requests are honored within a 30-day SLA. Records tagged recordsLawHold:true are exempt from auto-purge and dispositioned per the applicable records schedule. This task implements and tests both regimes before any real PII is stored.
objective: Implement the privacy-first constituent case model with PII minimization, AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption, a default 24-month retention window with auto-purge, a 30-day constituent/official deletion SLA, and the recordsLawHold exemption -- with tests covering both regimes.
acceptanceCriteria: Constituent case model fields limited to service needs: issue, status, next step, outcome, minimal contact; no political/affiliation/profiling fields exist in the schema
PII encrypted at rest; encryption key from environment, never committed
Default retention window (24 months from case closure) auto-minimizes/purges non-records data; tested with a fixture that advances the clock past the window
Constituent/official deletion path executes within a deletion SLA of 30 days; tested end-to-end
Records tagged recordsLawHold:true are exempt from auto-purge and from the deletion path; the retention job and deletion job both honor the flag; tested for both regimes
Privacy review sign-off and Expert (ethics/legal) sign-off recorded before merge
output: A merged PR with the privacy-first constituent case model, PII minimization by schema, AES-256-GCM encryption, retention-window auto-purge, deletion SLA path, and recordsLawHold exemption -- all tested.
